faqs.question: How can I get into Gajanan Maharaj College Of Pharmacy for UG/PG courses? Am I eligible for admission?
faqs.answer:
Gajanan Maharaj College Of Pharmacy has below criteria for taking admissions to it's various courses:
Courses Eligibility
B.Pharma Candidate must have studied Physics and Chemistry as compulsory subjects along with one of the Mathematics/Biology subjects with above mentioned marks in the above subjects taken together from any recognised Board/Council. The mentioned marks of SC/ST and OBC is for the candidate belonging to maharashtra State. Candidates who are appearing at the qualifying examination are also eligible. Maharashtra State candidates must have to appear for MHTCET. All India Candidates need to appear for MHTCET/NEET. However, preference shall be given to the candidate obtaining non zero positive score in NEET over the candidates who obtained non zero score in MHTCET.
D.Pharma Candidate passed 10+2 (science stream) with Physics, Chemistry and Biology or Mathematics or Any other qualification approved by the Pharmacy Council of India from any recognised institute/board are eligible to apply

reviews_list.review_title: Balanced academic experience with good Ayurvedic exposure and decent infrastructure.

review_content.Placements: Almost 90% of students get placements in college. The top recruiting companies for our college are Dabur, Himalaya, Baidyanath, etc. Almost all students get internships from my course. The role offered is junior residential doctor for freshers. The highest package offered is 12 LPA.
review_content.Infrastructure: Saraswati Ayurved Hospital and Medical College offers a range of facilities to the support both academic and personal development. The college campus is equipped with modern classrooms that are spacious, well-ventilated, and fitted with audio-visual aids to enhance learning.

reviews_list.batch_info: Bachelor of Ayurved Medicine and Surgery (B.A.M.S.) - Batch of 2026
reviews_list.review_title: College life is full of new adventures and experiences that give personality an incredible change.

review_content.Placements: Around 50% of students were placed in GMCH 32 Chandigarh but the package was just low. Top companies for recruitment are Himalaya and Shri Dhanvantri Herbals, Amritsar. Internships are done by students in hospitals like Indus Multispeciality Hospital. Students also get government PG admission.

reviews_list.review_title: Very Amazing College, fabulous teaching, and staff members are also good, hospital

review_content.Placements: 60 percent of students were placed in the previous batch. I don't know about the package because I am a first-year student. However, 100 percent of students got internships here. It's a good college for pursuing the BAMS course. Various roles are offered in our course.
review_content.Infrastructure: Facilities are good enough. Wi-Fi, labs, and classrooms are also good, and the library is nice. The quality of facilities in our hostel is good, but the area around the hostel is quite bad. The quality of food available in the mess is average, but the food quality is generally amazing.

reviews_list.batch_info: Bachelor of Ayurveda, Medicine and Surgery (BAMS) - Batch of 2028
reviews_list.review_title: It's okay to be in college to study your self chosen degree.

review_content.Placements: 100 seats were allotted last year when I came to this college. The highest package: 50-60k per month The lowest package: $20-30k per month First, we need to complete the 6-month internship program at the college itself. After then, some hospitals are offered to you. After completing this course, you are eligible to call yourself an authentic Ayurveda doctor. The main focus is to let everyone know about the importance of Ayurveda + modern allopathy too.
review_content.Infrastructure: Facilities given, such as neat and clean, well-organized labs, classrooms with AC, and a library, are very big and well managed by the attendant of the library itself. There is no wi-fi. The herbal garden is there in our college. Departments are there where you'll know how to make medicines. Teachers are very supportive. The hostel has so many restrictions, and the food quality is not so good, like for survival, you eat. Rooms are without AC, and if you want one, you have to pay extra for that. Plus point is no electricity bill is there. Outing closing time is 6 o'clock evening. Also, if you want to order something at night, you won't, because the gate is closed by 9:30 p.m.; otherwise, wardens are supportive. Canteen food is good; medical facilities are good also. Sports and games are like average. It depends on your interest. College as well as hostel conducts so many fiesta star nights, DJ nights, movie nights, and many more. So you never get bored.

faqs.question: Can I get direct admission at Karnataka State Akkamahadevi Women's University?
faqs.answer: Karnataka State Akkamahadevi Women's University offers admission solely on the basis of merit. The eligibility criteria varies for each course. However, the admission to all courses is based on the merit acquired in the qualifying exam as well as scores obtained in the course-level entrance exam. Candidates who wish to apply to KSWU, must fulfil the course-specific eligibility criteria.

faqs.question: Which entrance exams are accepted by Karnataka State Akkamahadevi Women's University?
faqs.answer: Karnataka State Akkamahadevi Women's University offers admission to several UG and PG courses. Admission to all these courses is based completely on the merit. Some courses also require an entrance exam in order to secure admission. KSAWUV only accepts CMAT, Karnataka PGCET, GUJCET, and KCET scores for entrance-based course admissions.

faqs.question: Can I join BSc at Karnataka State Akkamahadevi Women's University without 85% in PCM?
faqs.answer: Karnataka State Akkamahadevi Women's University offers admission to BSc courses solely on the basis of merit obtained in qualifying examination. However, the university has specified a minimum aggregate of 45% in Class 12. Candidates with 45% or more have a pretty good chance at getting into the university. However, candidate must have completed Class 12 from a recognised state or national board.

fees_and_eligibility.overview: offers a wide range of programmes to students at the UG and PG levels. The college is mainly concerned with stream such as Medicine & Health Sciences. At the UG level, the college offers courses such as B.Pharma and D.Pharma. At the PG level, the college offers courses such as M.Pharma. review_content.Placements: About 30-60% of students get placements directly from our college in reputed pharmaceutical companies. They get salary packages of nearly 2 LPA - 3 LPA. review_content.Infrastructure: Wi-Fi is available on campus and it works properly. Classrooms are air-conditioned but only a few work. Mess and accommodation facilities are provided in the hostel which is good for a normal student.

individual_reviews.title: Nice infrastructure and facilities, a smaller crowd,educated staff, and well-maintained surrounding.

content.Placements: About 5-7 percent of students get placement. The highest package was about INR 9 lacs per month, the lowest was INR 2 lacs, and the average is INR 4 lacs per month. Sun Pharma and Litmus Pharmaceuticals are the top recruiting companies. About 15 percent of students get internships in private pharmacies. Pharmacist, drug developer, and researcher are the roles offered.
content.Infrastructure: Labs, Wi-Fi, AC classrooms, and a library are available, but with fewer facilities, the labs are not well equipped, and the classrooms are not maintained regularly. Good food in the mess, sports tournament, spacious canteen, but with fewer food choices and not so clean as well.

individual_reviews.title: Dissatisfied because of the high fee structure.

This college is number 1 in terms of placements. 65% of students got placed in reputed companies like zZydus Pharma, Biochem, Laborate GSK etc. But internships are not provided in our program, and it is provided to degree students only. We only get training once after finishing the course.
content.Infrastructure: The college infrastructure is good. It has spacious and air-conditioned classrooms with proper lighting and a clean environment. They have gallery seating arrangements. Hostel facilities are available on the hostel premises in which two students stay in each room, which is not air-conditioned, but Wi-Fi is available. Food is provided by the college canteen, which is unhygienic and tasteless. No one wants to eat this. A library is present in one department, so other department students also walk in there, which is hard in summers.

review_content.Placements: After MD in psychiatry, you are at a very high level already, and it doesn't have anything to do with this institution. You can get a pretty good job in very renowned hospitals in India. The average salary package offered is around 10 LPA-11 LPA. If you have worked well, then you can get better salary packages, and your salary increases with time.
review_content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure and hostel here are good and green. The campus is less of campus and more of a forest which is a great thing. The classrooms are very old, but still in good condition. There is no canteen on the campus, and there is only a mess. There is only a badminton court inside the hostel in the name of sports. The mess cook makes good food, but he is not working now. He had a fight with some fellow students, and they stopped the mess service for them. From that time, we all are dependent on a maid who makes pathetic food, but we have no other option.

reviews_list.batch_info: MD in Psychiatry - Batch of 2020
reviews_list.review_title: Our college has the worst faculty members and a system.

review_content.Placements: Only 10 students are selected in our course out of 174 aspirants. The highest salary package offered to students is 13 LPA, and it increases with time. I am employed in a private hospital and approached it myself, so I do not have much idea of the companies. It all depends on the students. Companies don't come by themselves, and students have to reach out to them.
review_content.Infrastructure: Facilities like a hostel, mess, Wi-Fi, labs, and everything is so bad here. Anyone does not care about anything, and the authority does not take any responsibility. Our mess cook had a dispute with my mates, and they hired a maid, and she makes pathetic food. There is no Wi-Fi in the hostel. Classrooms are just average here. The library is good, but it could be better. There is only a badminton court inside the hostel.
